name: Enalapril
executiveSummary: Venous and arterial vasodilator. Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor.
indications:
## Main indications

Venous and arterial vasodilator. Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Tablets 5, 10 and 20 mg. Ampoule 1,25 mg/mL (limited IV use).
adulto.dose: Hypertension: 5–20 mg/day PO in 1–2 doses. Heart failure: start 2,5 mg PO 1–2 times/day and titrate.

pediatrico.dose: PO: Children: initial: 0,08 mg/kg/day every 24 h, maximum dose: < 12 years: 0,6 mg/kg/day; > 12 years: 40 mg/day; adolescents and adults: 2,5–5 mg/day, usual dose for hypertension: 10–40 mg/day. IV (enalaprilat): Children: 5–10 µg/kg/dose every 8–24 h; adolescents and adults: 0,625–1,25 mg/dose every 6 h.
pediatrico.presentation: Tablets: 5–10 mg; Oral solution (compounded): 2 mg/mL (0,1 mg/drop) See formulation
pediatrico.notes: With potassium-sparing diuretics may cause hyperkalemia. Caution in sodium-depleted patients. Dialyzable. See preliminary guide for prevention of medication-related teratogenesis.

stability:
## Stability

- Tablets: store per package insert at room temperature.
- IV solution: use immediately after preparation.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Cutaneous reactions, taste disturbances, vertigo, headache, neutropenia, orthostatic hypotension, cough. Teratogenic.
